# Verify a recipient can be given as a relative file:// path: the key file lives
# inside the repository so every admin resolves the same spec. Absolute file://
# paths are refused for exactly that reason.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# A relative file:// path is read through the repo root and recorded verbatim as
# the key's source in the audit log.
exec sesam tell --user peter --recipient file://keys/peter.pub --group dev
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"peter"'
stdout 'file://keys/peter.pub'

exec sesam verify

# An absolute file:// path is refused - other admins could not read it.
! exec sesam tell --user mallory --recipient file:///etc/hostname --group dev
stderr 'must be relative'

# A path escaping the repository via .. is confined by the repo root.
! exec sesam tell --user mallory --recipient file://../outside.pub --group dev

-- keys/peter.pub --
age1gtfhwme5a3sg9r7sp9vqu5awdzft4xd5qjesdq8lhry5wy8tfsfqw7fgt6
